references.title: A two-phase machine learning approach for predicting student outcomes

extracted.Courses: Courses in the CS department at a Greek University
extracted.Features: [GPA, Specialization Field, Capstone Project Grade, Time to Complete Basic Cycle, First Year Performance in Core Subjects, High School, Rank-in-Class, Student Mobility Program, Internship, Awards, Grade in High School, Grade in Entrance Exams, RIASEC/Holland code]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Preprocessing involves 3 main steps:

- Removing Ouliers - 2% of the datasset was removed this way

- Normalization

- Feature Extraction of FYPICS

The data is clustered using k-Means and each cluster has its own model
extracted.Comments:
Authors create a predictive model that is meant to predict how many years the student took finish the program.

This prediction is particularly trivial since it uses data from the entire program.

One approach that distinguishes this work from others though is that it clusters first and then predicts.

There is benchmarking of general model vs cluster-specific model
extracted.Limitations:
- System does not perform early prediction

- Limited preprocessing alternatives

- Task is too trivial

- Only considers academic profile data

extracted.Courses: Courses in the CS department at a Greek University

- The data is clustered using k-Means and each cluster has its own model
extracted.Comments:
Approach is related with the creation of specific models:

- Clustering in Supervised learning - specific cluster models. 

- SEIPS is about predicting whether the student will go into Post-graduate studies or not.

One approach that distinguishes this work from others though is that it clusters first and then classifies.

There is benchmarking of general model vs cluster-specific model
extracted.Limitations:
- System does not perform early prediction

- Limited preprocessing alternatives

- Task is too trivial

- Only considers academic profile data

extracted.Courses: Courses in the CS department at a Greek University
extracted.Features: [GPA, Specialization Field, Capstone Project Grade, Time to Complete Basic Cycle, First Year Performance in Core Subjects, High School, Rank-in-Class, Student Mobility Program, Internship, Awards, Grade in High School, Grade in Entrance Exams, RIASEC/Holland code]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Preprocessing involves 3 main steps:

- Removing Ouliers - 2% of the datasset was removed this way

- Normalization

- Feature Extraction of FYPICS
extracted.Cluster Description:
Cluster 1: Composed by close to 50% of students - these have the highest performance on first year (FYPICS) and finish their programs in the lowest time.

Cluster 2: Composed by close to 25% of learners. They place second.

Cluster 3: Composed by close to 20%, features the learners with llowest FYPICS and also the highest average TTD.
extracted.Implications: Clustering students before creating a classifier leads to overall better predictions than trying to classify students from the get go.
extracted.Comments: Clustering is used ass part of a more overarching framework called EDUC8.
extracted.Limitations:
- System does not perform early prediction

- Limited preprocessing alternatives

- Task is too trivial

- Only considers academic profile data

annotations.text: Paper presents a 2 phase machine learning approach to predict student outcomes in Higher Education:
Models achieve good accuracy, highlghting potential of these approaches for Learning Analytics.
